Infosys is a global leader in next-generation digital services and consulting. We enable clients in more than 50 countries to navigate their digital transformation. With over three decades of experience in managing the systems and workings of global enterprises, we expertly steer our clients through their digital journey. We do it by enabling the enterprise with an AI-powered core that helps prioritize the execution of change. We also empower the business with agile digital at scale to deliver unprecedented levels of performance and customer delight. Our always-on learning agenda drives their continuous improvement through building and transferring digital skills, expertise, and ideas from our innovation ecosystem.

Visit www.infosys.com to see how Infosys (NYSE: INFY) can help your enterprise navigate your next.

As Bindya S Raj, Infosys, says in a recent article, "With the right technology stack, implementation approach, and strategic partner, marketers can successfully transition to a modern, composable MarTech platform to become faster, smarter, adaptable, and gain further competitive edge."

Even as we look beyond Earth, it remains the only home we have.

At Infosys, this belief continues to guide our efforts. We have been carbon neutral for 6 years, achieved carbon neutrality 30 years ahead of the Paris Agreement, and reduced Scope 1 and 2 GHG emissions by 71%.

Our campuses reflect this commitment, with 100% wastewater recycled, 77.7% energy from renewable sources, and 60 MW of renewable energy in use. Through initiatives like 409 deep injection wells, we recharge over 20 million litres of water daily, while 407,000+ saplings planted in 2025 contribute to a greener future.

Recognized for climate leadership for 9 consecutive years, our focus now is to go beyond being carbon neutral and move towards becoming carbon positive.

Because there may be other worlds to explore, but there is only one we can truly live on.

#EarthDay #ESGIsAnOpportunity #Sustainability #ClimateAction

We have been made aware of forms of fraudulent correspondence/ potential scams claiming themselves to be associated with #InfosysFoundation or with any of our office bearers. Please be advised that Infosys Foundation, foundation employees, grantees, or partner organizations do not:

•	Request administrative fees for awarding a grant.
•	Solicit donations in any way, manner, or form at any time from the public.
•	Use social media to get beneficiaries.
•	Host internet lotteries or offer prizes of any kind through email, postal mail, telephone, fax, or in person.
•	Request registration fees for conferences or summits.
•	Provide memberships or request fees for memberships.
•	Approach individuals in-person offering grant opportunities.
•	Offer investment opportunities.
•	Communicate via generic E-mail addresses such as Gmail, Hotmail, Yahoo etc. All communications will always originate from a verifiable Infosys e-mail address (domain of ‘@infosys.com’) and not from any free web-based email accounts.

Stay alert and read more: https://infy.com/IwbGb

Swarnamala Prakash had always felt underconfident about her academics. English and mathematics seemed overwhelming, and without private tutoring, school was a constant struggle.

Her story mirrors that of hundreds of children across India. Fifteen years ago, Sanjeevani, our CSR group from the Thiruvananthapuram campus, launched Vijnana to change this. Every weekend, volunteers teach students from classes 5 to 10 subjects like mathematics, English, and general knowledge.

Even during the pandemic, our volunteers introduced both online and offline sessions to ensure learning never stopped. Today, classes run at three locations: a government school, a school for visually impaired children, and a children’s home.

But Vijnana goes beyond academics. Weekends are filled with storytelling, public speaking, drawing, and a ‘Kids’ Corner’ where children lead sessions. These interactions are true knowledge exchanges - volunteers learn as much as they teach. Over the years, thousands of lives have been touched.

Today, Swarnamala, who once feared English, looks forward to Saturdays - speaking fluently and spelling with ease.

On #NationalYouthDay, we celebrate the spirit of learning and empowerment that drives our educational initiatives. #InfosysFoundation and our volunteers work together to ensure marginalized students have access to education that unlocks opportunities and helps them thrive in an ever-changing world.

#InfosysCSR #Education
